fact: April 23 is exactly 113 days into the year - that's like counting to 113 Mississippi's without stopping!
In the Northern Hemisphere, dandelions are typically exploding everywhere right now, turning into those fluffy white puffballs you can blow wishes on.
Taurus season begins around now, which means the zodiac bull is charging in to replace Aries the ram - it's like a cosmic changing of the guard!
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, leaves are turning brilliant orange and red because it's actually autumn there while we're having spring.
Earthworms are usually having their busiest party of the year right now - the soil is finally warm and moist enough for them to wiggle around like crazy after winter.
The sun rises earlier and sets later every single day around April 23 in northern regions, adding about 2-3 minutes of extra daylight daily - like nature's own dimmer switch going up!
Cherry blossom petals are often falling like pink snow in many temperate regions, creating natural confetti carpets on sidewalks and streets.
April 23 falls almost exactly one month after the spring equinox, making it the perfect middle point of early spring when everything is waking up but not yet fully grown.
Frogs and toads are typically croaking their loudest love songs right now in northern climates because the weather is finally warm enough for them to find mates and lay eggs in ponds.
In northern regions, this is often prime maple syrup season - the sap is flowing fast through tree trunks as they wake up from winter sleep.
